text.title: Turborepo Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Common Turborepo mistakes: circular dependencies, missing task deps, caching non-deterministic tasks, phantom deps.

antipattern.description: Circular package dependencies: packages/a depends on packages/b, which depends on packages/a.
antipattern.reason: Turborepo cannot compute a valid execution order for a cycle; the build hangs or fails with an opaque error. Extract the shared code into a third package with no internal dependencies.

antipattern.description: Phantom dependencies: package uses a library that works in development because it is hoisted to the root `node_modules`, but the package.json does not declare it as a dependency.
antipattern.reason: The build works locally but fails on a clean install or after publishing because the transitive dep is no longer guaranteed to be present. Always declare every `import` as an explicit dependency.

antipattern.description: Hardcoded relative imports across packages: `import { Button } from '../../../packages/ui/src/button'`.
antipattern.reason: Relative cross-package imports bypass the exports map, break on publish, and are invisible to Turborepo's dependency graph. Always use the package name: `import { Button } from '@org/ui'`.

antipattern.description: Outputs path mismatch: `turbo.json` declares `outputs: ["dist/**"]` but the build tool emits to `out/`.
antipattern.reason: Turborepo caches an empty directory. Future CI runs get a cache hit with no files, then fail during the next task that consumes the outputs. Use `--summarize` to inspect what was actually cached.

antipattern.description: Undeclared environment variables in `env` / `globalEnv` that affect the build output.
antipattern.reason: The same task hash is computed for both `NODE_ENV=development` and `NODE_ENV=production` builds, so the prod artifact gets served as the dev build (or vice versa). Every env var that changes output must be declared.
